task: Learn Statistics & Probability
project: Analyse a real-world dataset (e.g. Titanic) and produce a statistical report
reason: Statistical thinking is the backbone of every ML algorithm.

task: Learn Machine Learning
project: Build a small project that uses Machine Learning
reason: Learning Machine Learning moves you closer to your career goal.

task: Learn Deep Learning
project: Build an image classifier using CNNs with PyTorch/TensorFlow
reason: Deep learning powers the most advanced AI systems today.

task: Learn Data Engineering
project: Build an ETL pipeline that ingests, cleans, and stores stock-market data
reason: Clean, reliable data pipelines are essential for production ML.

task: Learn Model Deployment
project: Deploy a sentiment-analysis model as a REST API using FastAPI + Docker
reason: Deployment skills bridge the gap between a model and real-world impact.

task: Learn LLM Systems
project: Build a RAG-based Q&A bot over your own PDF documents using LangChain
reason: LLM orchestration is the hottest skill in the current AI job market.

task: Learn Vector Databases
project: Build a semantic search engine for research papers using ChromaDB
reason: Vector databases are the storage layer of modern AI applications.

task: Learn MLOps
project: Set up a CI/CD pipeline for a ML model using GitHub Actions + MLflow
reason: MLOps practices ensure models stay reliable and maintainable in production.

task: Learn PyTorch
project: Implement a transformer from scratch to understand self-attention
reason: PyTorch is the dominant framework in AI research and industry.

task: Learn Transformers
project: Fine-tune a pre-trained BERT model for a custom text classification task
reason: Transformer architecture underpins virtually every modern LLM.

task: Learn Feature Engineering
project: Explore and engineer features on a Kaggle tabular dataset to beat a baseline
reason: Good features often matter more than model choice in real-world ML.

task: Analyse a real-world dataset (e.g. Titanic) and produce a statistical report
reason: Statistical thinking is the backbone of every ML algorithm.

task: Analyse the Titanic dataset: compute summary statistics, visualise distributions, and run a χ² test
reason: Statistical foundations are essential before tackling any ML algorithm.

task: Build a real-world advanced-level project that demonstrates practical mastery of Machine Learning
reason: Hands-on practice at the advanced level advances your journey towards Machine Learning mastery.

task: Develop a Python script that simulates data from different underlying distributions (e.g., Bernoulli, Uniform, Exponential) and then repeatedly samples from these populations to demonstrate and visually explain the Central Limit Theorem, showing how the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution regardless of the original data's distribution.
reason: This hands-on simulation reinforces foundational statistical concepts of sampling distributions and the Central Limit Theorem, crucial for understanding inferential statistics, confidence intervals, and various model assumptions in ML, building on previous exposure to data analysis.

task: Containerize a pre-trained advanced Machine Learning model (e.g., a multi-label image classifier or a complex NLP model), develop a production-ready RESTful API using FastAPI or Flask to serve real-time predictions, and deploy it to a cloud platform like AWS Sagemaker Endpoints or Google Cloud Run, ensuring basic monitoring and auto-scaling capabilities.
reason: This task moves beyond basic model deployment by requiring containerization, robust API development, and cloud-native scaling/monitoring, essential skills for an AI/ML Engineer deploying production systems.

task: Design and implement an advanced RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) system capable of answering complex, multi-hop questions over a private document corpus. Integrate techniques like query re-writing, hybrid search (keyword + semantic), and a re-ranking mechanism (e.g., using a cross-encoder). Develop a custom evaluation benchmark with a mix of factual and reasoning-based questions, and quantitatively measure the system's precision, recall, and relevance, justifying the choice of LLM and embedding models.
reason: Building on intermediate LLM understanding, this advanced project focuses on critical architectural patterns and evaluation methodologies for robust, production-grade LLM applications.

task: Build a contextual search and recommendation system for a large dataset (e.g., scientific papers, product catalog) by integrating a self-hosted (e.g., Qdrant, Milvus) or managed (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate) vector database. Your implementation should explore different embedding models (e.g., Sentence Transformers, custom fine-tuned) for ingestion, optimize indexing parameters, and develop an API that supports similarity search with metadata filtering and allows for comparing performance metrics across different configurations.
reason: This task moves beyond basic usage by requiring practical implementation, optimization, and performance comparison of vector database integrations for real-world applications.

task: Refactor the core prediction logic of your 'AI Learning Assistant' project into a production-ready RESTful API using FastAPI, containerize it using Docker, and deploy it to a cloud platform (e.g., Heroku free tier, Google Cloud Run) for real-time inference. Implement basic API key authentication for access control.
reason: This task builds upon your foundational understanding of model deployment and existing ML projects, providing practical experience in creating robust, scalable, and secure real-time inference endpoints, crucial for an AI/ML Engineer role.

task: Design and implement an advanced RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) system that integrates an LLM with a vector database (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, ChromaDB) to answer complex queries over a private document corpus. Extend this with an agentic workflow using a framework like LangChain or LlamaIndex, allowing the agent to use custom tools (e.g., web search, API calls) in addition to retrieval, and implement a qualitative/quantitative evaluation pipeline for hallucination detection and answer relevance.
reason: This task elevates your intermediate LLM knowledge by building a sophisticated RAG system with agentic capabilities and robust evaluation, demonstrating advanced proficiency in building intelligent conversational AI applications and addressing key industry trends.

task: Develop a web-based semantic search and Q&A application for a domain-specific dataset (e.g., academic papers, product documentation). Use a self-hosted or cloud-managed vector database (e.g., Milvus, Qdrant, Pinecone) to store embeddings generated from a suitable pre-trained embedding model (e.g., Sentence-BERT), and implement functionalities for document ingestion, semantic similarity search, and a simple LLM-powered Q&A interface over the retrieved context.
reason: This task transforms your foundational understanding of vector databases into practical application by building a functional semantic search and Q&A system, directly supporting advanced LLM applications like RAG and improving data retrieval expertise.

task: Build a production-ready API for a pre-trained machine learning model (e.g., a sentiment analysis or image classification model). Containerize the model using Docker, create a robust RESTful API with FastAPI or Flask, and deploy it to a cloud platform like AWS EC2/ECS or Google Cloud Run, implementing basic CI/CD for updates and monitoring.
reason: This task moves beyond theoretical learning of deployment to practical implementation, combining containerization, API development, cloud hosting, and basic MLOps practices, which are crucial for an AI/ML Engineer.

task: Design and implement an advanced Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) system capable of answering complex queries over a specialized, proprietary dataset (e.g., financial reports or medical research papers). The solution must incorporate advanced retrieval techniques (e.g., multi-stage retrieval, re-ranking with cross-encoders), demonstrate prompt engineering for robust responses, and include a mechanism for evaluating the quality and relevance of generated answers, finally deploying it as a scalable web application.
reason: Building on intermediate LLM knowledge and previous AI assistant projects, this challenges Aryan to tackle real-world RAG complexities, integrating advanced techniques, evaluation, and deployment, which are critical for current AI applications.

task: Develop a scalable semantic search engine for a large corpus of text documents (e.g., news articles, product descriptions). Implement an ETL pipeline to embed documents using a Transformer model and ingest them into a cloud-based vector database (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant). Design and build a search API that performs efficient similarity queries, and critically evaluate different vector indexing strategies for performance and recall.
reason: This moves beyond basic understanding to hands-on application, requiring Aryan to build a robust system, manage data pipelines, and analyze the performance implications of different vector database configurations, directly supporting advanced LLM system development.

task: Deploy a custom-trained ML model (from Aryan's "advanced-level project that demonstrates practical mastery of Machine Learning") as a scalable, containerized microservice API endpoint using Docker and a cloud platform (e.g., AWS ECS/EC2, GCP Cloud Run, Azure Container Apps). Implement basic API authentication, integrate a health check endpoint, and set up fundamental monitoring metrics (e.g., latency, error rates) for the deployed service.
reason: This task builds upon foundational model deployment knowledge by requiring practical application of containerization, cloud deployment, and basic MLOps practices like authentication and monitoring, directly advancing his ability to deploy scalable AI applications.

task: Design and implement a multi-document, advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system for a specific domain (e.g., legal documents, scientific papers). This system must incorporate multiple retrieval strategies (e.g., semantic search with a vector database, keyword search with BM25), a re-ranking module using a cross-encoder model, and an agentic query router that directs complex user questions to specialized sub-LLM chains or external tools. Develop a systematic evaluation pipeline for response quality, hallucination, and relevance using both automated metrics and human-in-the-loop validation.
reason: This task moves beyond basic LLM interaction by integrating sophisticated RAG techniques, agentic principles, and rigorous evaluation, which are critical for developing robust, intelligent, and production-ready LLM-powered applications.

task: Enhance an existing project (like the "AI Learning Assistant") or build a new intelligent search application by integrating a managed vector database service (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus Cloud). Implement a robust data ingestion pipeline to chunk, embed (using a suitable embedding model like `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` or `OpenAI Embeddings`), and store textual content from various sources into the vector database. Develop and evaluate efficient query strategies to retrieve relevant information based on semantic similarity, demonstrating how different indexing parameters or search algorithms impact retrieval performance.
reason: This task progresses beyond theoretical understanding by requiring practical integration of a managed vector database into an application, focusing on real-world data ingestion, embedding, and efficient semantic search for RAG systems, while also considering performance implications.
